edges.description: Diverse causal lesions converge on disrupted ovarian signaling and follicle maturation.
edges.source: Genetic disruption of female gonad development

edges.description: Chronic failure of follicle development and ovarian signaling produces streak or severely underdeveloped ovaries with loss of reproductive function.
Streak or severely underdeveloped dysfunctional ovaries are the defining gonadal dysgenesis phenotype.
Streak gonads and ovarian failure prevent normal ovulation and reproductive capacity.
intermediate_mechanisms: Loss of functional ovarian tissue prevents follicular maturation and ovulation.

edges.description: Ovarian failure lowers estrogen output and disinhibits pituitary gonadotropin secretion.
Ovarian steroidogenic failure drives compensatory pituitary gonadotropin elevation.
Estrogen deficiency from ovarian failure prevents spontaneous pubertal development.
Pubertal ovarian failure prevents menarche, producing primary amenorrhea.
Sustained hypoestrogenism limits uterine growth, producing uterine hypoplasia.

edges.source: Pubertal induction and long-term hormone replacement
Pubertal induction and long-term hormone replacement
Cyclic estrogen-progestogen maintenance when uterus is present
Audiology and hearing-support management for Perrault-spectrum cases

nodes.description: Perrault-spectrum patients need multidisciplinary hearing assessment and intervention planning in parallel with endocrine surveillance.
nodes.id: Audiology and hearing-support management for Perrault-spectrum cases

evidence.reference_title: LARS2-Perrault syndrome: a new case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: Hearing loss should be assessed and treated by a multidisciplinary team including an audiologist and otolaryngologist.

nodes.description: Once estrogenized uterine tissue is present, cyclic progestogen is used with estrogen to oppose endometrial effects during long-term replacement.
nodes.id: Cyclic estrogen-progestogen maintenance when uterus is present

evidence.reference_title: Misdiagnosis of Mullerian agenesis in a patient with 46, XX gonadal dysgenesis: a missed opportunity for prevention of osteoporosis.
evidence.snippet: Treatment with estrogen and progesterone are required for reducing the risk of endometrial hyperplasia and carcinoma, which will increase due to long-term application of estrogen without opposition.

nodes.description: Spontaneous pubertal development fails because ovarian estrogen production is inadequate.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor gene causes hereditary hypergonadotropic ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: We conclude that the mutation causes ODG in these families.

nodes.description: FSHR-related ovarian dysgenesis impairs follicle-stimulating hormone receptor signaling, producing follicular arrest with hypergonadotropic ovarian failure.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor gene causes hereditary hypergonadotropic ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: Expression of the gene in transfected cells demonstrated a dramatic reduction of binding capacity and signal transduction, but apparently normal ligand-binding affinity of the mutated receptor.

nodes.description: Streak gonads and ovarian failure prevent normal ovulation and reproductive capacity.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder characterized by lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism as a result of streak gonads.

nodes.description: Counseling should cover infertility expectations, reproductive endocrinology referral, donor-oocyte or gestational-surrogacy pathways when relevant, and recurrence-risk implications for inherited subtypes.

evidence.reference_title: Committee opinion no. 605: primary ovarian insufficiency in adolescents and young women.
evidence.snippet: Patients and their families should be counseled on the effect of the patient's condition on future fertility, on the risk of comorbidities associated with primary ovarian insufficiency, and on the condition's potential for genetic inheritance.

evidence.reference_title: Misdiagnosis of Mullerian agenesis in a patient with 46, XX gonadal dysgenesis: a missed opportunity for prevention of osteoporosis.
evidence.snippet: She has been informed about future options for having children by adoption or gestational surrogacy.

nodes.description: 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis is genetically heterogeneous, but the shared proximal lesion is failure of gene programs required for ovarian development and maintenance.
nodes.id: Genetic disruption of female gonad development

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: These results indicate a pivotal role for NUP107 in ovarian development and suggest that nucleoporin defects may play a role in milder and more common conditions such as premature ovarian failure.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in NR5A1 associated with ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mutations were associated with a range of ovarian anomalies, including 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis and 46,XX primary ovarian insufficiency.

nodes.description: Streak or severely underdeveloped ovaries are the defining structural lesion of the disease.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

nodes.description: Ovarian failure lowers sex-steroid output and drives compensatory gonadotropin elevation.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features of primary ovarian failure caused by a point mutation in the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor gene.
evidence.snippet: Clinically, both groups of patients were characterized by primary or early secondary amenorrhea, variable development of secondary sex characteristics, and high serum levels of FSH and LH.

nodes.description: Loss of ovarian steroidogenic function results in estrogen deficiency and compensatory elevation of pituitary gonadotropins, driving pubertal failure and amenorrhea.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical features of primary ovarian failure caused by a point mutation in the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor gene.
evidence.snippet: Clinically, both groups of patients were characterized by primary or early secondary amenorrhea, variable development of secondary sex characteristics, and high serum levels of FSH and LH.

nodes.description: A major shared mechanistic branch is reduced ovarian hormone-response and follicular maintenance, including impaired FSH signaling and defective estrogen-responsive transcription.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation in the follicle-stimulating hormone receptor gene causes hereditary hypergonadotropic ovarian failure.
evidence.snippet: Expression of the gene in transfected cells demonstrated a dramatic reduction of binding capacity and signal transduction, but apparently normal ligand-binding affinity of the mutated receptor.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: Impaired estrogenic signaling can lead to ovarian dysgenesis both by affecting the size of the follicular pool created during fetal development and by failing to counteract follicular atresia during puberty.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in NR5A1 associated with ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Mutations were associated with a range of ovarian anomalies, including 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis and 46,XX primary ovarian insufficiency.

nodes.description: NR5A1-related ovarian insufficiency reflects impaired transcriptional activation of steroidogenic and follicle-growth genes in ovarian cells, with variable penetrance and expressivity across families.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in NR5A1 associated with ovarian insufficiency.
evidence.snippet: Our data show that mutated forms of NR5A1, detected in subjects with anomalies of ovarian development and function, show quantitative impairment in the transactivation of two of these factors (CYP11A1 and CYP19A1).

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: Using homozygosity mapping and whole-exome sequencing, we identified a recessive missense mutation in nucleoporin-107 (NUP107, c.1339G>A, p.D447N).

nodes.description: NUP107-related ovarian dysgenesis links a nuclear pore component to sex-specific defective oogenesis and ovarian development in 46,XX individuals.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: NUP107 is a component of the nuclear pore complex, and the NUP107-associated protein SEH1 is required for oogenesis in Drosophila.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: In Drosophila, Nup107 knockdown in somatic gonadal cells resulted in female sterility, whereas males were fully fertile.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: Affected females were homozygous for a 3 bp deletion (NM_016556.2, c.600_602del) in the PSMC3IP gene, leading to deletion of a glutamic acid residue (p.Glu201del) in the highly conserved C-terminal acidic domain.

nodes.description: PSMC3IP/HOP2-related disease disrupts estrogen-driven nuclear-receptor coactivation, reducing fetal follicular-pool establishment and increasing vulnerability to follicular atresia during puberty.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: In cell lines, the p.Glu201del mutation abolished PSMC3IP activation of estrogen-driven transcription.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: Impaired estrogenic signaling can lead to ovarian dysgenesis both by affecting the size of the follicular pool created during fetal development and by failing to counteract follicular atresia during puberty.

nodes.description: Menarche does not occur because ovarian failure prevents normal pubertal maturation.

evidence.reference_title: XX ovarian dysgenesis is caused by a PSMC3IP/HOP2 mutation that abolishes coactivation of estrogen-driven transcription.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder characterized by lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism as a result of streak gonads.

nodes.description: Estrogen-based pubertal induction followed by estrogen-progestogen maintenance is used to initiate and sustain puberty, support secondary sexual characteristics, and reduce long-term consequences of hypoestrogenism.
nodes.id: Pubertal induction and long-term hormone replacement

evidence.reference_title: Hypogonadism in adolescent girls: treatment and long-term effects.
evidence.snippet: Girls with hypogonadism require hormone replacement therapy to initiate and sustain puberty.

evidence.reference_title: A rare case of 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis, Mayer-Rokitansky-Kuster-Hauser syndrome, pituitary and thyroid hypoplasia.
evidence.snippet: Hormone replacement therapy remains the only therapeutic option for the development of secondary sexual characteristics and the prevention of osteoporosis.

nodes.description: Sensorineural hearing impairment distinguishes Perrault-spectrum disease from isolated 46,XX gonadal dysgenesis and should prompt audiology and syndromic genetic evaluation.

evidence.reference_title: LARS2-Perrault syndrome: a new case report and literature review.
evidence.snippet: Perrault syndrome (MIM: 233400) is a rare recessive genetically heterogeneous disorder characterized by sensorineural hearing loss in males and females and ovarian dysfunction in females [1].

nodes.description: The convergent anatomical outcome is a streak or severely underdeveloped ovary with little or no effective steroidogenic or follicular function.

evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.

evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder characterized by lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism as a result of streak gonads.

nodes.description: Uterine growth remains poor when ovarian estrogen production is absent or severely reduced.

evidence.reference_title: A mutation in the nucleoporin-107 gene causes XX gonadal dysgenesis.
evidence.snippet: XX female gonadal dysgenesis (XX-GD) is a rare, genetically heterogeneous disorder that is characterized by underdeveloped, dysfunctional ovaries, with subsequent lack of spontaneous pubertal development, primary amenorrhea, uterine hypoplasia, and hypergonadotropic hypogonadism.
